title: GitHub repository configuration and .github directory
description: Set up the .github/ directory with issue templates, a pull request template, and a SECURITY.md file. Configure repository metadata so that the GitHub presence is professional and welcoming to potential contributors or clubs evaluating the project.
acceptance_criteria: Bug report issue template exists with structured fields (environment, steps to reproduce, expected/actual behaviour)
Feature request issue template exists with fields for the use case and proposed solution
Club adaptation request issue template exists — a lightweight way for other parks league clubs to express interest
Pull request template exists with checklist (description, testing, screenshots if UI)
SECURITY.md exists with responsible disclosure instructions pointing to James Hartt's contact

approach: 2. Write bug report template using GitHub issue template frontmatter (name, about, labels)
4. Write club enquiry template — friendly, low-friction, no jargon. Title like 'Interested in using jim.tennis for your club?' with fields for club name, league, and what they're looking for
5. Write PR template with a sensible checklist
6. Write SECURITY.md with James Hartt's preferred contact for security issues
technical_notes.design_notes: The club enquiry template is deliberately low-key — it's just an issue template, not a sales page. Anyone browsing the repo who thinks 'could this work for us?' has an obvious, frictionless way to ask.

testing_requirements: All templates render correctly on GitHub (correct frontmatter, labels, descriptions)
Issue template chooser shows all three options when creating a new issue

context.user_story: As a developer or club representative visiting the GitHub repository, I want clear templates so I know how to report issues, suggest features, or enquire about using the system for my club
